channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas school district apologizes for sending student data to wrong families
item.description: AISD confirmed that a link including students' data is now inactive, and any families who received the data cannot access it.
item.dc:creator: Brynne Herzfeld, NewsNation

item.title: AirPods dying? Professor discovers microenvironment that may be responsible
item.description: UT-Austin associate professor Yijin Lu said a similar microenvironment exists in all sorts of electronics. This includes places like your phone and electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: Retired US military leaders support letting Israel 'finish the job' against Iran, proxies
item.description: Dozens of retired U.S. military leaders urge President Donald Trump to throw America's support behind Israel in its fight against a nuclear Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Virginia hospital to continue gender-affirming care in light of Trump's order
item.description: The executive order was aimed at withholding federal funding for institutions who provide gender-affirming care for those under 19.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia daycare owner speaks after toddler wanders from facility
item.description: Monique Ede, owner of Fortress of God Childcare in Chester has spoken exclusively with 8News after a child wandered off from her facility.
item.dc:creator: Rolynn Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: More endangered Mexican gray wolves are roaming the southwestern US, annual survey shows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It is a ritual that takes place every winter — federal and state wildlife managers use remote cameras, scat collection, radio telemetry devices and helicopters to count Mexican gray wolves that are roaming mountain ranges in parts of New Mexico and Arizona.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hundreds of college students volunteer to clean up the McKellar Lake shoreline
item.description: "I like the feeling of making a tangible difference because a lot of what we do is in the future," said Louisiana State University student Nevaeh Barriere.
item.dc:creator: Mike Suriani, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted sandwiches on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch. The chain said it is offering this free food giveaway to show appreciation for customers’ patience as it perfected the new product.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Hateful message left on Ohio restaurant receipt results in outpouring of support, staff says
item.description: The public is rallying behind a central Ohio Mexican restaurant after a customer left a hateful message instead of a tip.
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman, Fox 5

item.title: Powerful US storms bring threats ranging from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms tore roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the country Tuesday with weather ranging from fire in the Southern High Plains to blizzards in the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas to host World Sign Spinning Championship
item.description: “It’s really neat because on an average day, when we’re out spinning for a business, we’ll spin for close to 20,000 people, that’s about how many cars drive by in a day.
item.dc:creator: JC Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson tells GOP lawmakers to skip town halls after an onslaught of protests
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is encouraging Republican lawmakers to skip town halls that have been rife with protesters.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jon Stewart accepts Elon Musk's demands for 'Daily Show' interview, fires back at his criticism
item.description: Jon Stewart is rejecting Elon Musk's criticism that he's a "propagandist," and added that he's "game" for an interview with the tech billionaire if he agrees to appear as a guest on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Accused Texas cop killer returns to court after Dem judge freed him on bond in ‘slap in the face’ move: expert
item.description: Dremone Francis, the Texas man accused of killing Deputy Fernando Esqueda in July 2024, is set to appear in court Tuesday for the first time since he was released on bond.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: AP again seeks end of its White House ban, saying the Trump administration is retaliating further
item.description: For a second time, the Associated Press is asking a federal judge to immediately restore its access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 9 states report measles cases as outbreaks grow
item.description: One of the most contagious viruses in the world is spreading around the United States. So far in 2025 there have been three distinct measles outbreaks and at least 164 cases reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention coming from nine states.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer mice with thick hair like the extinct woolly mammoth
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Extinction is still forever, but scientists at the biotech company Colossal Biosciences are trying what they say is the next best thing to restoring ancient beasts — genetically engineering living animals with qualities to resemble extinct species like the woolly mammoth.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon orders thousands more troops to southern border
item.description: The Pentagon is sending up to 5,050 additional troops to the U.S.-Mexico border even as unlawful border crossings have sharply fallen in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: Companies that depend on cross-border trade were already facing higher prices in anticipation of the 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada that took effect Tuesday. Now disruption looms, and if consumers won’t accept steep price hikes, they’ll have to downsize operations and lay off workers.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tesla charging stations in Massachusetts 'intentionally set' on fire, police say
item.description: Seven Tesla superchargers were damaged by a fire in Massachusetts that investigators say was intentionally set, as they look for a suspect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to block Mexico's $10 billion lawsuit against US gun makers
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed likely Tuesday to block a $10 billion lawsuit Mexico filed against top firearm manufacturers in the U.S. alleging the companies have fueled cartel gun violence.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen, as he continued his more than two-week long fight against double pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, WGN 9
